LAW AND MOTION, 302, NO APPEARANCE ON THIS MATTER. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING. DEFENDANTS DOMINIC MASSONI AND MARLOW & MASSONI'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT IS OVERRULED. PLAINTIFF HAS PLED SUFFICIENT FACTS TO ALLEGE DAMAGES AND THE ACCRUAL OF THE ACTION. (SEE FIRST AM. COMPLAINT, 10, 11.) ON THE FACE OF THE FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT, THE ACTION IS NOT CLEARLY BARRED BY THE STATUTE OF LIMITATIONS PURSUANT TO CCP 339(1). (SEE FAC, 14.) AS PLED, THE FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT IS NOT BARRED BY THE DEFENSE OF LACHES AS PLAINTIFF SEEKS COMPENSATORY RELIEF, AND NOT EQUITABLE RELIEF. (5 WITKIN, CAL. PROC. (5TH ED. 2008) PLEAD, 963, P. 376 ("WHERE THE ACTION SEEKS EQUITABLE RELIEF ALONE, AND UNDER THE FACTS ALLEGED IN THE COMPLAINT THE DEFENSE OF LACHES (PREJUDICIAL DELAY) WOULD BE A BAR TO A JUDGMENT FOR THE PLAINTIFF, THE COMPLAINT FAILS TO STATE A CAUSE OF ACTION AND A GENERAL DEMURRER WILL LIE.") (PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER.) JUDGE: ERNEST H. GOLDSMITH, CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT ; COURT REPORTER: SHERRY SAWYER, CSR # 5976

Off Calendar Feb-11-2015 Continued To Apr-08-15 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings
Off Calendar Feb-20-2015 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES: Atty. JENNIFER FRANSEN GOULD of Garvey, Schubert, Barer Law, Tel No. (503) 228-3939 appearing for Plaintiff Barbara Cavanaugh, Executor of the Estate of Agnes M. Carey;
Atty. MALCOLM LEADER-PICONE of Bartlett, Leader-Picone & Young, LLP, Tel No. (510) 444-2404 appearing for Defendant Dominic Massoni, CPA.

RULING - A R G U E D - The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT DOMINIC MASSONI'S Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings is denied. It is not necessary that a complete cause of action exist at the time of death in order for rights to survive that may later mature into an actionable claim. (Carr v. Progressive Cas. Ins. Co. (1984) 152 Cal.App.3d 881, 891.) In addition, whether, when, and to what extent the Decedent was injured is a fact issue not appropriate for resolution on the pleadings. Further, as pled, the causes of action are not barred by the statute of limitations. As determined on Defendant's previous Demurrer, the FAC adequately alleges delayed discovery. (FAC 14.) Defendant cites nothing in the prove-up papers that alters or undermines the relevant allegations in the FAC. Finally, the damages for attorneys' and accountants' fees incurred to correct and mitigate damages resulting from Defendant's alleged negligence are proper damages in a malpractice action. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk: Sean Kane; Reporter: Melanie Dawn Gheno, CSR #7489 (302/EHG)

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Feb-05-2014 Overruled
MINI MINUTES:

No appearance on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling.
DEFENDANTs DOMINIC MASSONI and MARLOW & MASSONI's DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT is Overruled. Plaintiff has pled sufficient facts to allege damages and the accrual of the action. (See First Am. Complaint, 10, 11.) On the face of the First Amended Complaint, the action is not clearly barred by the statute of limitations pursuant to CCP 339(1). (See FAC, 14.) As pled, the First Amended Complaint is not barred by the defense of laches as Plaintiff seeks compensatory relief, and not equitable relief. (5 Witkin, Cal. Proc. (5th ed. 2008) Plead, 963, p. 376 ("Where the action seeks equitable relief alone, and under the facts alleged in the complaint the defense of laches (prejudicial delay) would be a bar to a judgment for the plaintiff, the complaint fails to state a cause of action and a general demurrer will lie.") (Prevailing party to prepare a form of order.) Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith, Clerk: Cynthia Herbert ; Court Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR # 5976=(302/EHG)
